title: Scoring simulated SAINT input with the pure-R engine
text: define two real baits (BaitA, BaitB) two replicates , plus two controls. Six preys exist; BaitA enriches Prey1/Prey2 BaitB Prey3/Prey4. remaining preys background.
code: library(saintexpress)  simulate_si <- function(seed = 42, mode = c("spc", "int")) {   mode <- match.arg(mode)   set.seed(seed)   preys  <- paste0("Prey", 1:6)   baits  <- c("BaitA", "BaitA", "BaitB", "BaitB", "Ctrl1", "Ctrl2")   ips    <- paste0("IP", seq_along(baits))   cort   <- c("T", "T", "T", "T", "C", "C")    draw <- function(bait, prey) {     if (bait %in% c("Ctrl1", "Ctrl2")) {       if (mode == "spc") stats::rpois(1, 0.5) else stats::rexp(1, 1 / 1e4)     } else if (bait == "BaitA" && prey %in% c("Prey1", "Prey2")) {       if (mode == "spc") stats::rpois(1, 20) else stats::rexp(1, 1 / 1e7)     } else if (bait == "BaitB" && prey %in% c("Prey3", "Prey4")) {       if (mode == "spc") stats::rpois(1, 15) else stats::rexp(1, 1 / 5e6)     } else {       if (mode == "spc") stats::rpois(1, 0.5) else stats::rexp(1, 1 / 1e4)     }   }    rows <- list()   for (i in seq_along(baits)) {     for (p in preys) {       q <- draw(baits[i], p)       if (q > 0) {         rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(           ipId = ips[i], baitId = baits[i], preyId = p, quant = q,           stringsAsFactors = FALSE         )       }     }   }   list(     inter = do.call(rbind, rows),     prey  = data.frame(preyId = preys, preyLength = 500L, preyGeneId = preys,                        stringsAsFactors = FALSE),     bait  = data.frame(ipId = ips, baitId = baits, CorT = cort,                        stringsAsFactors = FALSE)   ) }  si_spc <- simulate_si(mode = "spc") str(si_spc, max.level = 2) #> List of 3 #>  $ inter:'data.frame':   19 obs. of  4 variables: #>   ..$ ipId  : chr [1:19] "IP1" "IP1" "IP1" "IP1" ... #>   ..$ baitId: chr [1:19] "BaitA" "BaitA" "BaitA" "BaitA" ... #>   ..$ preyId: chr [1:19] "Prey1" "Prey2" "Prey4" "Prey6" ... #>   ..$ quant : int [1:19] 26 17 1 1 22 22 2 2 15 10 ... #>  $ prey :'data.frame':   6 obs. of  3 variables: #>   ..$ preyId    : chr [1:6] "Prey1" "Prey2" "Prey3" "Prey4" ... #>   ..$ preyLength: int [1:6] 500 500 500 500 500 500 #>   ..$ preyGeneId: chr [1:6] "Prey1" "Prey2" "Prey3" "Prey4" ... #>  $ bait :'data.frame':   6 obs. of  3 variables: #>   ..$ ipId  : chr [1:6] "IP1" "IP2" "IP3" "IP4" ... #>   ..$ baitId: chr [1:6] "BaitA" "BaitA" "BaitB" "BaitB" ... #>   ..$ CorT  : chr [1:6] "T" "T" "T" "T" ...

title: Scoring simulated SAINT input with the pure-R engine
Scoring simulated SAINT input with the pure-R engine
text: true interactors (Prey1/Prey2 BaitA, Prey3/Prey4 BaitB) land top bait AvgP:
code: scores_spc <- run_saint(si_spc, mode = "spc", optimizer = "base") scores_spc[, c("Bait", "Prey", "AvgP", "BFDR", "SaintScore")] #>    Bait  Prey AvgP BFDR SaintScore #> 1 BaitA Prey1 1.00 0.00       1.00 #> 2 BaitA Prey2 1.00 0.00       1.00 #> 3 BaitA Prey4 0.00 0.26       0.00 #> 4 BaitA Prey6 0.05 0.12       0.05 #> 5 BaitA Prey5 0.38 0.00       0.38 #> 6 BaitB Prey3 1.00 0.00       1.00 #> 7 BaitB Prey4 1.00 0.00       1.00 #> 8 BaitB Prey2 0.00 0.26       0.00 #> 9 BaitB Prey6 0.00 0.26       0.00 top_per_bait <- function(df) {   df <- df[order(df$Bait, -df$AvgP), ]   do.call(rbind, by(df, df$Bait, head, 2)) } top_per_bait(scores_spc)[, c("Bait", "Prey", "AvgP", "BFDR")] #>          Bait  Prey AvgP BFDR #> BaitA.1 BaitA Prey1    1    0 #> BaitA.2 BaitA Prey2    1    0 #> BaitB.6 BaitB Prey3    1    0 #> BaitB.7 BaitB Prey4    1    0

title: Scoring simulated SAINT input with the pure-R engine
code: si_int <- simulate_si(mode = "int") scores_int <- run_saint(si_int, mode = "int", optimizer = "base") top_per_bait(scores_int)[, c("Bait", "Prey", "AvgP", "BFDR")] #>           Bait  Prey AvgP BFDR #> BaitA.1  BaitA Prey1    1    0 #> BaitA.2  BaitA Prey2    1    0 #> BaitB.9  BaitB Prey3    1    0 #> BaitB.10 BaitB Prey4    1    0

title: Pure R Implementation of SAINTexpress
text: saintexpress provides pure-R implementation SAINTexpress scoring engines spectral counts intensities. Documentation: https://prolfqua.github.io/saintexpress/ package intentionally focused R implementation. ship run native SAINTexpress binaries. Native execution handled separate saintexpressbin package, prolfqua integration handled prolfquasaint.

title: Pure R Implementation of SAINTexpress
Pure R Implementation of SAINTexpress
text: Prepare SAINT input list three data frames named inter, prey, bait: Validate input shape: Run spectral-count engine: Run intensity engine: result data frame SAINTexpress list.txt output shape.
code: si <- list(   inter = inter,   prey = prey,   bait = bait ) saintexpress::validate_saint_input(si) result <- saintexpress::run_saint(si, mode = "spc") result <- saintexpress::run_saint(si, mode = "int")

title: Pure R Implementation of SAINTexpress
text: default optimizer base R: optional nloptr optimizer can used nloptr package installed:
code: saintexpress::run_saint(si, mode = "spc", optimizer = "base") saintexpress::run_saint(si, mode = "spc", optimizer = "nloptr")

title: Pure R Implementation of SAINTexpress
text: worked example simulated AP-MS data included package vignette: walks validate_saint_input() run_saint() spc int modes 6-prey/4-bait synthetic experiment known true interactors, mirrors structure companion saintexpressbin vignette two engines can compared side side.

title: Pure R Implementation of SAINTexpress
text: package uses small synthetic inputs unit tests. TIP49 reference fixtures native-vs-R comparison tests live prolfquasaint, saintexpress saintexpressbin integrated.

title: Pure R Implementation of SAINTexpress
text: SAINTexpress SAINT model described original publications: Teo G, Liu G, Zhang J, Nesvizhskii AI, Gingras AC, Choi H (2014). SAINTexpress: improvements additional features Significance Analysis INTeractome software. Journal Proteomics 100:37-43. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jprot.2013.10.023 Choi H, Glatter T, Gstaiger M, Nesvizhskii AI (2012). SAINT-MS1: protein-protein interaction scoring using label-free intensity data affinity purification-mass spectrometry experiments. Journal Proteome Research 11:2619-2624. https://doi.org/10.1021/pr201185r Choi H, Larsen B, Lin ZY, Breitkreutz , Mellacheruvu D, Fermin D, Qin ZS, Tyers M, Gingras AC, Nesvizhskii AI (2011). SAINT: probabilistic scoring affinity purification-mass spectrometry data. Nature Methods 8:70-73. https://doi.org/10.1038/nmeth.1541

what: Run the pure-R SAINTexpress engine — run_saint
title: Run the pure-R SAINTexpress engine — run_saint
Run the pure-R SAINTexpress engine — run_saint
Run the pure-R SAINTexpress engine — run_saint
text: si named list elements inter, prey, bait (data frames). mode "spc" spectral counts, "int" intensities. optimizer "base" (default; uses stats::optim()) "nloptr" (uses NLopt COBYLA via optional nloptr package). ... Additional arguments forwarded internal scorers saint_spc_r() saint_int_r() (e.g. R, L, frequency, p2p_mapping).

title: Run the pure-R SAINTexpress engine — run_saint
